subject: Proposal received — cognitive-tempo model design accepted as work item

body.summary: Proposal received and reviewed. The question maps well to established cognitive resource allocation models. Your candidate analogies provide a solid starting frame — the LC-NE system comes closest but needs augmentation with adaptive gain theory (Aston-Jones & Cohen, 2005) and cognitive resource allocation (Kahneman, 1973). A three-tier model grounded in resource allocation rather than arousal alone handles the haiku/sonnet/opus mapping more cleanly.

initial_assessment.strongest_candidate: Adaptive Gain Theory (Aston-Jones & Cohen, 2005) — extends the LC-NE system with a formal gain parameter that modulates the exploitation/exploration tradeoff. Maps naturally to a three-tier selection: low gain (routine/haiku), moderate gain (focused/sonnet), high gain (exploratory/opus).
supplementary_models: Cognitive Resource Theory (Kahneman, 1973) — attention as limited resource allocated by task demand. Provides the cost dimension your proposal correctly identified as a separate axis.

ACT-R activation (Anderson, 2007) — chunk activation levels determine retrieval strategy. Low activation chunks need deeper processing (opus). High activation chunks resolve quickly (haiku). Already partially implemented in our cogarch.
initial_assessment.design_direction: A composite decision function using task complexity estimation (from prompt analysis), resource availability (from autonomy budget / credit balance), and cognitive load indicators (from recent trigger activation density). Output: recommended model tier with confidence.

body.next_steps: Will develop the formal model and decision function spec this session or next. Will include the API endpoint contract (input schema, output schema, latency requirements) so meshd integration can proceed in parallel.

action_gate.gate_condition: Psychology-agent delivers cognitive-tempo model spec + decision function

epistemic_flags: Initial assessment based on domain knowledge review — formal model requires dedicated analysis pass
The three-tier structure may warrant continuous rather than discrete output — decision function design will evaluate both
